dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6778 IH: Parkway Safety and Reinvestment Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Beyer introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources

form.official-title: To authorize the Secretary of the Interior to use revenue collected from speed safety cameras on highways in the National Park System for maintenance and construction purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Parkway Safety and Reinvestment Act.

section.header: Speed safety cameras in the National Park System

subsection.text: Subject to subsection (d), if a vehicle is recorded by a speed safety camera while being operated in violation of an applicable traffic regulation for a covered highway, the Secretary may, with respect to the responsible party—

paragraph.text: after providing the responsible party with notice and opportunity for a hearing on the record, assess a civil penalty.

subsection.text: Notwithstanding section 1402 of the Victims of Crime Act of 1984 (34 U.S.C. 20101), the Secretary may collect and expend, without subsequent appropriation, any revenues obtained by the Federal Government from speed safety camera citations issued pursuant to subparagraph (a) for—

paragraph.text: the construction and maintenance of the covered highways and parking facilities of the unit of the National Park System in which the citation was issued; and
the installation, repair, and maintenance of speed safety cameras.

subsection.text: In carrying out this Act, the Secretary may enter into a contract or other appropriate agreement with a person for the purposes of installing, repairing, maintaining, or replacing speed safety cameras.
In carrying out this Act, the Secretary may only use a speed safety camera in accordance with the State law of the State in which the applicable portion of covered highway is located.

paragraph.text: The term covered highway means a highway—

subparagraph.text: administered by the Secretary of the Interior; and
located within a unit of the National Park System.

paragraph.text: The term highway has the meaning given the term in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of section 101(a)(11) of title 23, United States Code.
The term Secretary means the Secretary of the Interior.

The term speed safety camera means a camera that captures a still or moving image of a vehicle for the purpose of traffic enforcement, and does not include handheld radar and other devices operated by law enforcement officers to make an on-the-scene traffic stop, issue a traffic citation, or other enforcement action at the time of the violation.

The term speed safety camera citation means a traffic citation processed by an automated traffic enforcement system owned or operated by the National Park Service for a traffic violation occurring on the covered highway.
The term State has the meaning given such term in section 31 of title 18, United States Code.
